rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VENRICE PALMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/21/2021 to allow time to process filings and provide required proposed order and courtesy copies. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/7/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSE ANGEL MARQUEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/7/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: What is meant by notice "filed concurrently with this Petition" in paragraphs 6 and 7 of the petition? The register of actions does not show any notice filed on 3/23/21. A petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.36 I.2.] Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. Does the executor have proof of the allowed claim having been paid? The Court cannot find and order what has not been disclosed in a verified pleading or prayed. The executor states that no real property taxes are due and payable by the estate but the proposed order has a finding that all real property taxes due and payable by the estate have been paid. Likewise, the executor states in paragraph 24 of her petition that the attorney fees will be paid out of the estate assets, but paragraph 12 of the proposed order findings states that the fee is to be paid outside of estate assets. And the petition does not include an omnibus clause. There is no Exhibit A to the proposed order (and there should not be one), but the full legal description of the real property is missing from the proposed order. The executor is the sole beneficiary. She does not need to give notice to herself or any longer to heirs who are not beneficiaries. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/27/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MADELINE H. DE HAVEN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for the court to determine whether to grant the petition and unwind the 1998 order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEWIS E. AND MARGARET E. BELL TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/21/2021 first available date. 1) Petitioner must give 30 days' notice on the mandatory judicial council form DE-115. (See Probate Code 851 and CCP 413.10). All heirs at law need be listed and receive the notice of hearing. 2) Please state petitioners address and explain why venue is proper in San Francisco when both co-trustees Michael Bell and John Bell appear to live in Daly City. (See Probate Code 17002). This is a threshold issue that must be determined prior to the petition being adjudicated. 3) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [See CRC 7.902]. If there are any minor contingent beneficiaries please provide legal authority for whether a guardian ad litem should be appointed. 4) Page 2 of the petition lines 27-28 allege, "We are submitting a trust and correct copy of the complete trust subsequently by declaration by the trustee." Please explain and file a complete copy as no filed Declarations by the trustee appear to include this. 5) Please file attorney timesheets for the requested $15,370.00 that comply with local rules. 6) Please prove the alleged accounts that are sought to be confirmed as assets of the trust were in existence prior to execution of the trust. 7) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/7/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 3 different amendments filed that appear to attempt to add Petitioner's information to the petition. Waivers filed by Jovita and Richard wave formal notice of hearing on this petition but does not specify the title of the petition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN L. VALENTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 1/21/2010. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required, notwithstanding the slightly incorrect publication that referred to an amended petition when an amendment to the petition was filed. The Notice of Amendment to the Petition, however, appears to have been correct; it is assumed that the newspaper chose the incorrect wording. Status of closing the estate will be set on 8/17/2022. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 8/17/2022. Appearance will be required on 8/17/2022 if no petition has been filed.
